intended_use: EchoConfidence is Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) that displays images from a Transthoracic Echocardiogram, and assists the user in reviewing the images, making measurements and writing a report.
intended_use_full: EchoConfidence is Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) that displays images from a Transthoracic Echocardiogram, and assists the user in reviewing the images, making measurements and writing a report. The intended medical indication is for patients requiring review or analysis of their echocardiographic images acquired for their cardiac anatomy, structure and function. This includes automatic view classification; segmentation of cardiac structures including the left and right ventricle, chamber walls, left and right atria and great vessels; measures of cardiac function; and Doppler assessments.
target_condition: Cardiac anatomy, structure and function assessment; patients with known or suspected underlying cardiac disease
ground_truth: Reference standard derived from three independent human expert readers who performed measurements as if in clinical use; a physician reviewed and adjusted approximately 10% of measurements as would occur in clinical practice.
other_metrics: Mean Absolute Error (MAE) comparison: AI MAE versus 3 human expert MAE; primary acceptance criterion was upper 95% confidence interval of the difference <+25% (expressed as percentage of human expert MAE). Point estimates reported to show AI agreeing with humans better than humans agree with each other; 95% confidence intervals reported as <0% and well below +25% criterion across overall cohort and 20 demographic subgroups. Bland-Altman analysis reported based on averages of human expert measurements.
